name: European plum (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (raw ripe Prunus domestica); concentration_mM assumes fruit density ~1 g/mL (100 g ~ 100 mL)

components.name: D-Glucose
components.evidence: free glucose 5.1 g; the single largest carbon source in ripe plum

components.name: Sucrose
components.evidence: sucrose 1.6 g; varietal range up to ~4.9 g (Zhang et al. 2024, PMC11408021)

components.name: D-Sorbitol
components.source: Zhang et al. 2024 Food Chem X, PMC11408021 (sorbitol mean 15.07% of total sugar, 0.86-29.81 mg/g FW)
components.evidence: sorbitol is a defining fermentable polyol of Prunus; not counted in USDA total sugars. Representative ~1.0 g/100g, cultivar range ~0.3-3.0 g

components.name: L-Malate (malic acid)
components.source: Zhang et al. 2024 PMC11408021 (malic 177.6-1568.4 mg/100g, mean 73.7% of total acids)
components.evidence: DOMINANT organic acid and a major supplementary carbon/energy source; representative 0.8 g, cultivar range 0.18-1.57 g

components.name: Quinic acid
components.source: Zhang et al. 2024 PMC11408021 (quinic 11.0-420.3 mg/100g, mean 16.44% of total acids)
components.evidence: second most abundant acid in plum flesh; range 0.01-0.42 g

components.name: L-Aspartate
components.evidence: DOMINANT free/total amino acid (measured as Asp incl. asparagine); the principal organic-N source of plum

components.name: Sulfate (S)
components.evidence: present via S-amino acids; not separately quantified

components.name: Gamma-Tocopherol
components.evidence: present, trace; not quantified in USDA panel

components.name: Retinol (preformed vitamin A)
components.evidence: 0 ug (plant food; vitamin A supplied as provitamin carotenoids)

components.name: Beta-Carotene
components.evidence: beta-carotene 190 ug (provitamin A; total vitamin A 17 ug RAE)

components.name: Alpha-Carotene
components.evidence: 0 ug in plum (retained from FooDB list but USDA reports absent)

components.name: Total dietary fiber
components.evidence: 1.4 g; includes pectin (galacturonan) - fermentable by many gut microbes

remove.name: Silver
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic metalloid contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: cobalt is ultratrace in plum flesh and not a measured nutrient; FooDB noise

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: ultratrace contaminant element, not a defined plum nutrient

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: ultratrace, not a quantified plum nutrient

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: ultratrace, not quantified in plum; drop as noise

remove.name: Iodine c
remove.reason: plums contain negligible/undetectable iodine; FooDB noise

remove.name: Starch C12H20O10
remove.reason: ripe plum starch is ~0 g/100g (USDA); removed as effectively absent

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: duplicate compartment-tagged iron entry; consolidated into single Iron

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: duplicate compartment-tagged iron entry; consolidated into single Iron

remove.name: Aluminum
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace/contaminant element

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: trace plant element, not a microbial growth nutrient; drop as noise

remove.name: Silicon
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element

remove.name: Strontium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Barium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Titanium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant element

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element

remove.name: Lanthanum
remove.reason: non-nutritive rare-earth contaminant element

remove.name: Phlorizin
remove.reason: apple-characteristic dihydrochalcone; FooDB cross-contamination, not a plum constituent

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: microbial fermentation product / food preservative additive, not a natural plum compound

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: predicted stereoisomer artifact, not a measured metabolite

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin typical of Apiaceae/citrus; implausible in plum flesh, likely FooDB predicted

missing_added: specific fermentable sugars with amounts (glucose 5.1, fructose 3.1, sucrose 1.6, galactose 0.14, maltose 0.08 g/100g)
D-sorbitol (defining fermentable polyol of Prunus, ~1.0 g/100g) - previously absent
organic acids with amounts: malic (dominant, 0.8 g), quinic (0.17 g), citric, tartaric, oxalic, succinic
complete amino acid profile with amounts (aspartate dominant at 0.35 g/100g)
full mineral panel with amounts (K 157 mg dominant)
full vitamin panel with amounts (C, B1/B2/B3/B5/B6, folate, choline, K, E)
keep_uncovered: Neochlorogenic acid (dominant plum phenolic; no BiGG met)
Procyanidin B1/B2/B3/B4/B5/B7/C1/trimer EEC (genuine plum flavan-3-ol oligomers)
Cyanidin 3-glucoside / Cyanidin 3-rutinoside / Cyanidin / Peonidin 3-glucoside / Peonidin 3-rutinoside (plum-skin anthocyanins)
Amygdalin (trace cyanogenic glycoside genuine to Prunus)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 169949 (Plums, raw; SR Legacy) - full macro/sugar/AA/mineral/vitamin/fatty-acid panel
Zhang et al. 2024, Food Chemistry: X, 'Soluble sugar, organic acid and phenolic composition and flavor evaluation of plum fruits', PMC11408021 - quantitative organic acids and sorbitol
Milosevic et al. 2022, chemical composition of European plum 'Cacanska Lepotica', PMC9498388 - organic-acid corroboration
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are free glucose (5.1 g) > fructose (3.1 g) > sucrose (1.6 g), supplemented by sorbitol (~1.0 g) and malic acid (~0.8 g, the dominant organic acid). Principal organic-N source is aspartate/asparagine (0.35 g). Organic-acid and sorbitol values are cultivar-variable (ranges noted); all sugar/AA/mineral/vitamin/fatty-acid values from USDA FDC 169949.
